title: Configure phase versus generate phase
description: CMake first runs the configure step that executes CMakeLists logic, then a generate step that emits build files where generator expressions are resolved.
body: Use when reasoning about when a value is known. Do not expect generator expressions to have a value during configure. The configure phase runs commands, sets variables, and populates the cache; the generate phase writes the native build system and evaluates genex against the concrete configuration. Inspect configure-time values with message and generate-time values with file(GENERATE). Validation signals include configure-time logic computing inputs and genex selecting per-config outputs correctly. Risk boundary: mixing the two phases produces flags applied to the wrong configuration, so keep per-config decisions in genex.
